trivia_bullets.text: Innocuous Rat sits in the black creature suite of Duskmourn: House of Horror (DSK, 2024-09-27), a set built around the atmosphere of a single supernatural haunted house. The rat is a horror-movie staple — a mundane animal that signals something wrong nearby.
sources: Wizards.com: Planeswalker's Guide to Duskmourn, via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: Carries Manifest Dread, a DSK-original mechanic that upgrades original Manifest (Fate Reforged, 2015). Unlike original Manifest which blindly placed the top card face down, Manifest Dread lets the controller look at the top two cards, choose which becomes the face-down 2/2 and which goes to the graveyard — adding card selection and Delirium fueling simultaneously.
sources: Wizards.com: Duskmourn House of Horror Mechanics (Matt Tabak, 2024-08-31)
DotEsports: MTG manifest dread mechanic in Duskmourn explained

trivia_bullets.text: As a 1/1 for {1}{B} that makes a 2/2 on death and mills a card, Innocuous Rat was assessed at DSK release as a usable Pauper blocker with limited competitive ceiling — manifested cards only pay off if flippable creatures land face down. EDHREC rank 11,625.
sources: CoolStuffInc: Duskmourn House of Horrors Pauper Review by Paige Smith, via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: Illustrated by Maxime Minard, a French concept artist with recent MTG credits.
